codeReview.summary: Well-implemented GGUF export module that converts PyTorch BitNet ternary weights to GGUF format. The implementation correctly uses TQ1_0 quantization for FFN BitLinear layers (w_gate, w_up, w_down) and F32 for embeddings, norms, and MLA attention projections. All 32 tests pass covering absmean quantization, GGUF validity, metadata presence, ternary weight fidelity, model component coverage, end-to-end export, and config defaults. The code follows project conventions (type annotations, docstrings, dataclass config, pytest), passes ty check and ruff check. Key architectural mapping between PyTorch state dict keys and GGUF BITNET tensor names is correct and verified against the actual model.py source.

issues.description: MLA down-projection layers (w_down_q, w_down_kv) are not exported to GGUF. These nn.Linear layers compress input to latent space before up-projection to Q/K/V during inference. The export only maps w_up_q, w_up_k, w_up_v, and w_out. While the feature's expectedBehavior lists 'BitLinear layers, embeddings, lm_head, norm layers' (not explicitly MLA projections), these weights are needed for complete inference. The test checkpoint includes them but silently drops them during export. Recommend adding export mapping for MLA compression layers in a follow-up, or documenting that they are excluded by design.

create_minimal_gguf() does not include position embeddings (pos_embd), but export_to_gguf() does export them when present in the state dict. This means create_minimal_gguf produces a GGUF file missing a tensor that export_to_gguf would include. Tests using create_minimal_gguf don't verify position embedding export. Minor since the main export path is tested separately via _create_test_checkpoint which includes position embeddings.

The GGUF_MAGIC constant (0x46554747) is defined but never used in the module. The test file has its own _read_gguf_header function that reads the magic number. Consider either using the constant in the module or removing it to reduce dead code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that the gguf Python library has TQ1_0 quantization type (gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.TQ1_0) with gguf.QK_K=256 block size. The library handles padding automatically when the last dimension isn't a multiple of 256. This is important knowledge for any future workers working with GGUF ternary quantization.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: gguf_export.py:287-311 — _quantize_ternary_to_tq1_0 function with manual padding and gguf.quantize() call

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker confirmed that the GGUF bitnet architecture tensor naming convention uses: token_embd, pos_embd, output (lm_head), output_norm, blk.{i}.attn_norm/q/k/v/output/sub_norm, blk.{i}.ffn_norm/gate/up/down. This naming is specific to the bitnet architecture in GGUF and differs from e.g. llama architecture names.

Worker followed the AGENTS.md conventions correctly: uv for package management, ruff for linting, ty for type checking, pytest for tests, dataclass for config, pathlib.Path for paths, type annotations throughout, CPU-only tests. No deviations from procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read AGENTS.md first, used uv run pytest, ty check, ruff check. All code follows documented conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The model's state dict uses .gamma for RMSNorm (custom implementation, not nn.LayerNorm) and .weight for BitLinear and nn.Linear. MLA uses nn.Linear with standard .weight parameters. Router weights are intentionally excluded from GGUF export.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: model.py:99 — self.gamma = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(d_model)) for RMSNorm; bitlinear.py uses self.weight; mla.py uses nn.Linear

summary: The gguf-export feature is well-implemented and passes all review criteria. The GGUF export module correctly converts PyTorch BitNet ternary weights to GGUF format using TQ1_0 quantization for BitLinear FFN layers and F32 for other components. All 32 unit tests pass, covering absmean quantization, file validity (magic number, gguf library readability), metadata presence (model name, context length, embedding dim, vocab size, architecture), ternary weight fidelity (TQ1_0 round-trip), model component coverage (embeddings, norms, attention, FFN, lm_head), end-to-end export from checkpoint, and config defaults. The implementation correctly maps the DrLLMModel state dict keys to GGUF BITNET architecture tensor names. Static analysis (ty check, ruff check, ruff format) all pass. Non-blocking issues: MLA down-projection layers (w_down_q, w_down_kv) are not exported, and create_minimal_gguf omits position embeddings.
